findings.title: No Conditional Access Policies Enabled
findings.description: No enabled Conditional Access policies were found. Access to M365 resources is not context-aware and cannot enforce MFA, compliant devices, or location restrictions.
findings.recommendation: Deploy a baseline set of Conditional Access policies covering MFA for all users, MFA for admins, block legacy authentication, and require compliant devices.

findings.title: Legacy Authentication Not Blocked
findings.description: Legacy authentication protocols are not blocked. These protocols bypass MFA and are a primary vector in password spray and credential stuffing attacks.
findings.recommendation: Create a Conditional Access policy to block all legacy authentication. Audit any applications or workflows that rely on legacy auth before enforcing.

findings.title: SharePoint Sharing Set to Anyone
findings.description: SharePoint and OneDrive external sharing is set to Anyone, allowing unauthenticated link sharing. Files can be shared with anyone on the internet without sign-in.
findings.recommendation: Restrict external sharing to New and existing guests at minimum. Review and tighten sharing settings per site collection where appropriate.

findings.title: Low MFA Coverage
findings.description: Fewer than 95% of licensed users have MFA registered. This significantly increases account compromise risk via phishing or credential stuffing.
findings.recommendation: Enable MFA for all users via Conditional Access. Consider enabling Security Defaults if no CA policies are currently in place.

findings.title: Auto-Forwarding to External Recipients Allowed
findings.description: Automatic email forwarding to external recipients is not blocked. This is a common data exfiltration technique used following account compromise.
findings.recommendation: Block external auto-forwarding via the outbound spam filter policy or a mail flow transport rule.

findings.title: Mailbox Auditing Not Fully Enabled
findings.description: Mailbox auditing is not enabled across all mailboxes. Without audit logs, it is not possible to investigate suspicious activity or data access.
findings.recommendation: Enable organisation-wide mailbox auditing using Set-OrganizationConfig -AuditDisabled $false.

findings.title: Excessive Global Administrators
findings.description: Seven Global Administrators were found. Global Admin is the highest-privilege role in M365 and should be assigned to a maximum of 2-3 break-glass accounts.
findings.recommendation: Reduce Global Admins to 2-3 emergency-access accounts. Assign least-privilege roles such as Exchange Admin or SharePoint Admin for day-to-day tasks.

findings.title: Low Device Compliance Rate
findings.description: Fewer than 80% of managed devices are compliant with Intune compliance policies. Non-compliant devices may lack encryption, current OS patches, or required security configuration.
findings.recommendation: Review non-compliant devices in the Intune portal. Identify common compliance failures and remediate. Consider blocking non-compliant device access to M365 via Conditional Access.

findings.title: Teams External Access Not Restricted
findings.description: Teams external access is open to all domains. Users can communicate with any external Teams tenant, increasing the risk of phishing or data leakage.
findings.recommendation: Restrict Teams external access to approved domains only, or disable federation if not required for business operations.

findings.title: High Number of Guest Accounts
findings.description: A large number of guest accounts exist in the tenant. Unreviewed guests may retain access to data long after a project or relationship has ended.
findings.recommendation: Implement an Entra ID access review policy for guest accounts. Remove guests who no longer require access.

findings.title: Anti-Phishing Intelligence Not Enabled
findings.description: Mailbox intelligence in the anti-phishing policy is not enabled, reducing protection against targeted impersonation attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enable mailbox intelligence and impersonation protection in the Defender for Office 365 anti-phishing policy.

findings.title: No Privileged Identity Management
findings.description: Privileged Identity Management is not in use. All admin role assignments are permanent, increasing the risk of privilege abuse or account compromise.
findings.recommendation: Enable Microsoft Entra PIM and convert permanent admin role assignments to eligible just-in-time assignments.

findings.title: High Number of Unused Licences
findings.description: 26% of purchased licences are unassigned, representing unnecessary recurring cost.
findings.recommendation: Audit unassigned licences and remove from the subscription where no longer required. Review licence assignment processes.

findings.title: No Intune Compliance Policies Configured
findings.description: No device compliance policies are configured in Intune. Without compliance policies, devices cannot be evaluated or blocked from accessing M365 resources.
findings.recommendation: Create compliance policies for each managed device platform covering minimum OS version, encryption, and antivirus requirements.

findings.title: Teams Consumer Access Enabled
findings.description: Users can communicate with Teams personal and consumer accounts, increasing data leakage risk.
findings.recommendation: Disable Teams consumer access unless there is a specific approved business requirement.
